Question: Find the value of $$\int_{C} ( x + y ) d s ,$$ where $$C$$ is the curve parameterized by $$x = t ,$$ $$y = t ,$$ $$0 \le t \le 1 .$$

Evaluate $$\int_{C} ( x^{2} + y^{2} + z ) d s ,$$ where C is the curve with parameterization $$\textbf{r} \left(\right. t \left.\right) = \langle \text{sin} \left(\right. 3 t \left.\right) , \text{cos} \left(\right. 3 t \left.\right) ,\text{t} \rangle , 0 \leq t \leq 2 \pi .$$

Evaluate line integral $$\int_{C} ( x^{2} + y z ) d s ,$$ where $$C$$ is the line with parameterization $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = \langle 2 t , 5 t , \text{unicode[:-]} t \rangle , 0 \le t \le 10 .$$ Reparameterize C with parameterization $$\mathbf{\text{s}} ( t ) = \langle 4 t , 10 t , -2 t \rangle , 0 \le t \le 5 ,$$ recalculate line integral $$\int_{C} ( x^{2} + y z ) d s ,$$ and notice that the change of parameterization had no effect on the value of the integral.
Find the length of a wire with parameterization $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = \langle 3 t + 1 , 4 - 2 t , 5 + 2 t \rangle , 0 \le t \le 4 .$$

Let $$\mathbf{\text{F}} = x \mathbf{\text{i}} + y \mathbf{\text{j}}$$ be a vector field and let C be the curve with parameterization $$\langle t , t^{2} \rangle$$ for $$0 \le t \le 2 .$$ Which is greater: $$\int_{C} \mathbf{\text{F}} \cdotp \mathbf{\text{T}} d s$$ or $$\int_{\text{unicode[:-]} C} \mathbf{\text{F}} \cdotp \mathbf{\text{T}} d s ?$$

Find the value of $$\int_{C} 4 x d x + z d y + 4 y^{2} d z ,$$ where $$C$$ is the curve parameterized by $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = \langle 4 \text{cos} ( 2 t ) , 2 \text{sin} ( 2 t ) , 3 \rangle , 0 \le t \le \frac{\pi}{4} .$$

Calculate line integral $$\int_{C} \mathbf{\text{F}} \cdotp d \mathbf{\text{r}} ,$$ where F is vector field $$\langle y^{2} , 2 x y + 1 \rangle$$ and C is a triangle with vertices $$( 0 , 0 ) ,$$ $$( 4 , 0 ) ,$$ and $$( 0 , 5 ) ,$$ oriented counterclockwise.

Calculate the mass of a spring in the shape of a helix parameterized by $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = \langle \text{cos} t , \text{sin} t , t \rangle , 0 \le t \le 6 \pi ,$$ with a density function given by $$\rho ( x , y , z ) = x + y + z$$ kg/m.
Calculate the flux of $$\mathbf{\text{F}} = \langle x + y , 2 y \rangle$$ across the line segment from $$( 0 , 0 )$$ to $$( 2 , 3 ) ,$$ where the curve is oriented from left to right.
Calculate the circulation of $$\mathbf{\text{F}} ( x , y ) = \langle - \frac{y}{x^{2} + y^{2}} , \frac{x}{x^{2} + y^{2}} \rangle$$ along a unit circle oriented counterclockwise.
Calculate the work done by field $$\mathbf{\text{F}} ( x , y ) = \langle 2 x , 3 y \rangle$$ on a particle that traverses the unit circle. Assume the particle begins its movement at $$( -1 , 0 ) .$$
